sections.heading: Character Filter Types
sections.body:
MongoDB Search supports the following types of character filter:

- [htmlStrip](htmlStrip-ref)

- [icuNormalize](icuNormalize-ref)

- [mapping](mapping-ref)

- [persian](persian-ref)

The following sample index definitions and queries use the [sample collection](custom-analyzers-eg-coll) named `minutes`.
To follow along with these examples, load the `minutes` collection on your cluster
and navigate to the Create a Search Index page in the Atlas UI following the steps
in the [Create a MongoDB Search index](create-fts-index) tutorial.
Then, select the `minutes` collection as your data source, and follow the example procedure
to create an index from the Atlas UI or using [mongosh](mongodb-shell).

drivers

***

➤ Use the **Select your language** drop-down menu to set the
method to run the examples on this page.

***

sections.heading: htmlStrip
sections.body: The `htmlStrip` character filter strips out HTML constructs.

sections.heading: Attributes
sections.body:
The `htmlStrip` character filter has the following attributes:

| Name        | Type             | Required? | Description                                                                               |
| ----------- | ---------------- | --------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| type        | string           | yes       | Human-readable label that identifies this character filter type. Value must be htmlStrip. |
| ignoredTags | array of strings | yes       | List that contains the HTML tags to exclude from filtering.                               |

sections.heading: Example

codeBlocks.content:
SCORE: 0.32283568382263184 _id:  “2”
 message: "do not forget to SIGN-IN. See ① for details."
 page_updated_by: Object
   last_name: "OHRBACH"
   first_name: "Noël"
   email: "ohrbach@example.com"
   phone: "(123) 456 0987"
 text: Object
   en_US: "The head of the sales department spoke first."
   fa_IR: "ابتدا رئیس بخش فروش صحبت کرد"
   sv_FI: "Först talade chefen för försäljningsavdelningen"

SCORE: 0.3076632022857666 _id:  “3”
 message: "try to sign-in"
 page_updated_by: Object
   last_name: "LEWINSKY"
   first_name: "Brièle"
   email: "lewinsky@example.com"
   phone: "(123).456.9870"
 text: Object
   en_US: "<body>We'll head out to the conference room by noon.</body>"

sections.heading: icuNormalize
sections.body:
The `icuNormalize` character filter normalizes text with the [ICU](http://site.icu-project.org/) Normalizer. It is based on Lucene's
[ICUNormalizer2CharFilter](https://lucene.apache.org/core/8_3_0/analyzers-icu/org/apache/lucene/analysis/icu/ICUNormalizer2CharFilter.html).

sections.heading: Attributes
sections.body:
The `icuNormalize` character filter has the following attribute:

| Name | Type   | Required? | Description                                                                                  |
| ---- | ------ | --------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| type | string | yes       | Human-readable label that identifies this character filter type. Value must be icuNormalize. |

sections.heading: Example

codeBlocks.content:
SCORE: 0.4923309087753296  _id:  “4”
 message: "write down your signature or phone №"
 page_updated_by: Object
   last_name: "LEVINSKI"
   first_name: "François"
   email: "levinski@example.com"
   phone: "123-456-8907"
 text: Object
   en_US: "<body>This page has been updated with the items on the agenda.</body>"
   es_MX: "La página ha sido actualizada con los puntos de la agenda."
   pl_PL: "Strona została zaktualizowana o punkty porządku obrad."

sections.heading: mapping
sections.body:
The `mapping` character filter applies user-specified normalization
mappings to characters. It is based on Lucene's [MappingCharFilter](https://lucene.apache.org/core/8_0_0/analyzers-common/org/apache/lucene/analysis/charfilter/MappingCharFilter.html).

sections.heading: Attributes
sections.body:
The `mapping` character filter has the following attributes:

| Name     | Type   | Required? | Description                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| -------- | ------ | --------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| type     | string | yes       | Human-readable label that identifies this character filter type.Value must be mapping.                                                                                                                |
| mappings | object | yes       | Object that contains a comma-separated list of mappings. A mapping indicates that one character or group of characters should be substituted for another, in the format \<original> : \<replacement>. |

sections.heading: Example

codeBlocks.content:
SCORE: 0.5472603440284729 _id:  “1”
 message: "try to siGn-In"
 page_updated_by: Object
   last_name: "AUERBACH"
   first_name: "Siân"
   email: "auerbach@example.com"
   phone: "(123)-456-7890"
 text: Object
   en_US: "<head> This page deals with department meetings.</head>"
   sv_FI: "Den här sidan behandlar avdelningsmöten"
   fr_CA: "Cette page traite des réunions de département"

sections.heading: persian
sections.body:
The `persian` character filter replaces instances of [zero-width
non-joiner](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zero-width_non-joiner)
with the space character. This character filter is based on Lucene's
[PersianCharFilter](https://lucene.apache.org/core/8_0_0/analyzers-common/org/apache/lucene/analysis/fa/PersianCharFilter.html).

sections.heading: Attributes
sections.body:
The `persian` character filter has the following attribute:

| Name | Type   | Required? | Description                                                                             |
| ---- | ------ | --------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| type | string | yes       | Human-readable label that identifies this character filter type. Value must be persian. |

sections.heading: Example

SCORE: 0.13076457381248474  _id:  “2”
 message: "do not forget to SIGN-IN. See ① for details."

   fa_IR: "ابتدا رئیس بخش فروش صحبت کرد"
   sv_FI: "Först talade chefen för försäljningsavdelningen"

sections.heading: Learn More
sections.body:
To see additional index definitions and queries that use the `mapping` character filter, see the following reference page examples:

- [shingle](shingle-tf-ref) token filter

- [regexCaptureGroup](regexCaptureGroup-tokenizer-ref) tokenizer
